title: Sione Mata'utia
wikitext:
{{short description|Australia & Samoa international rugby league footballer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2015}} {{Use Australian English|date=March 2015}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Sione Mata'utia |fullname = Sione Mata'utia-Leifi |image = |imagesize = |caption = |birth_date = {{birth date and age|df=yes|1996|6|25}} |birth_place = [[Liverpool, New South Wales|Liverpool]], New South Wales, Australia |height = {{convert|1.88|m|ftin|abbr=on|order=flip}} |weight = {{convert|104|kg|stlb|abbr=on|order=flip}} |position = {{rlp|SR|CE}} |club1 = {{nowrap|[[Newcastle Knights]]}} |year1start = 2014 |year1end = 20 |appearances1 = 124 |tries1 = 31 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 124 |club2 = [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] |year2start = 2021 |year2end = 24 |appearances2 = 85 |tries2 = 17 |goals2 = 1 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 70 |teamA = [[Prime Minister's XIII]] |yearAstart = 2014 |yearAend = |appearancesA = 1 |triesA = 0 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 0 |teamB = [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] |yearBstart = 2014 |yearBend = |appearancesB = 3 |triesB = 1 |goalsB = 0 |fieldgoalsB = 0 |pointsB = 4 |teamC = [[NRL All Stars team|World All Stars]] |yearCstart = 2017 |yearCend = |appearancesC = 1 |triesC = 0 |goalsC = 0 |fieldgoalsC = 0 |pointsC = 0 |teamD = [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] |yearDstart = 2017 |yearDend = |appearancesD = 1 |triesD = 0 |goalsD = 0 |fieldgoalsD = 0 |pointsD = 0 |retired = |source = {{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/sione-mata-utia/summary.html|title=Sione Mata'utia - Career Stats & Summary|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2016-03-20}} |updated = 1 November 2024 | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Chanel Mata'utia]] (brother)
[[Pat Mata'utia]] (brother)
[[Peter Mata'utia]] (brother)
[[Masada Iosefa]] (cousin) }} }} '''Sione Mata'utia-Leifi''' (pronounced ma-ta-oo-tee-a){{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/07/24/double_debut_for_mata_utias.html|title=Double debut for Mata'utias - Knights|date=24 July 2014|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-05-03}} (born 25 June 1996) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward and {{rlp|ce}} for [[Lakes United Seagulls]] in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]]. He has represented both [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at international level. He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], as well as the [[Prime Minister's XIII]] and [[NRL All Stars team|World All Stars]] at representative level. Mata'utia played as a {{rlp|fb|wg}}er and {{rlp|ce}} earlier in his career, before moving into the forwards. ==Background== Mata'utia was born in [[Liverpool, New South Wales]], Australia. He is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent and moved to [[Newcastle, New South Wales]] when he was 5 years old.{{cite web|author=Michael Carayannis|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/sione-matautia-may-yet-back-out-of-agreement-to-join-canterbury-bulldogs-20141011-114qg3.html#ixzz3FproNCdG|title=Sione Mata'utia may yet back out of agreement to join Canterbury Bulldogs|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=2014-10-11|access-date=2015-05-03}}{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/members-only/knights_insider_sione_mata_utia.html|title=KNIGHTS INSIDER: Sione Mata'utia - Knights|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-05-03}} He played his junior football for the [[Raymond Terrace Magpies]] and [[South Newcastle Lions]] in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]], before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]]. Mata'utia is the younger brother of former Knights players [[Peter Mata'utia]], [[Chanel Mata'utia]] and [[Pat Mata'utia]].{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/07/23/sione_mata_utia_set_to_debut.html|title=Sione's set to debut - Knights|publisher=newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2014-07-25|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141030121857/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/07/23/sione_mata_utia_set_to_debut.html|archive-date=30 October 2014|df=dmy-all}} ==Playing career== ===Early career=== In 2013 and 2014, Mata'utia played for Newcastle's [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/m/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150622220803/http://nycdatabase.org/players/m/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=22 June 2015|title=M|date=27 December 2012|publisher=Nycdatabase.org|access-date=2015-06-26}} On 16 October 2013, he extended his contract with the Knights from the end of 2014 to the end of 2015.{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2013/10/16/matautia-trio-plus-tetevano-re-sign.html|title=Mata'utia trio plus Tetevano re-sign - Knights|date=16 October 2013|publisher=newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2014-07-25}} On 12 June 2013, he won the [[Brian Carlson|Brian Carlson Club]]-[[Andrew Johns]] Medal as Newcastle's best under-17 player.{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2013/06/12/junior-presentation-night-winners.html|title=Junior Presentation Night Winners - Knights|publisher=newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2014-07-25}} In September and November 2013, he captained the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]].{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsportspulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=1-2130-0-0-0&sID=26416&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=25953696|title=Australians Win Second Test - Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League - FOX SPORTS PULSE|publisher=foxsportspulse.com|access-date=2014-07-25}}{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com/assrl_2013_archives.php|title=ASSRL Schoolboys rugby league news, Australian Secondary School Rugby League, Australian Schoolboys|publisher=ourfootyteam.com|access-date=2014-07-25}} ===2014=== On 3 May, Mata'utia played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] Under-20s team against the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] Under-20s team, playing at centre and scoring a try in New South Wales' 30-8 win at [[Penrith Stadium]].{{cite web|author=Brad Walter|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/mitchell-moses-stars-as-nsw-win-under-20s-origin-clash-20140503-zr41x.html|title=Mitchell Moses stars as NSW win under 20s Origin clash|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=2014-05-03|access-date=2015-05-03}} In round 20 of the [[2014 NRL season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Newcastle against the [[Sydney Roosters]] at fullback,{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/07/25/late_mail_knights_v_roosters.html|title=LATE MAIL: Knights v Roosters - Knights|date=25 July 2014|publisher=newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2014-07-25}} alongside his brother [[Chanel Mata'utia]] who also debuted for the Knights in the same game.{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/07/24/double_debut_for_mata_utias.html|title=Double debut for Mata'utias - Knights|date=24 July 2014|publisher=newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2014-07-25}} This was the first time since Round 1 of the [[1942 NSWRFL season]], that two brothers had debuted in the same game together, [[Bill McRitchie|Bill]] and [[Doug McRitchie]] debuting for the [[St. George Dragons]] on that day.{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/2440794/brothers-sione-and-chanel-matautia-make-history-with-knights-debut/?cs=306|title=Brothers Sione and Chanel Mata'utia make history with Knights debut | Newcastle Herald|publisher=theherald.com.au|access-date=2014-07-25}} In round 23 against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] at [[Hunter Stadium]], he scored his first NRL career try in a three try effort for Newcastle during their 28-22 win.{{cite web|url=http://m.nrl.com/matautia-brothers-sink-warriors/tabid/10874/newsid/81085/default.aspx|title=Mata'utia brothers sink Warriors - NRL.com|publisher=M.nrl.com|date=2014-08-17|access-date=2015-05-03}} He finished off his debut year in the NRL having played in seven matches and scoring seven tries for Newcastle in the 2014 season.{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY0NC00LS0tLS0tMjA4NjYtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-10-18|access-date=2015-12-25}} On 2 September, he was named at centre in the 2014 NYC Team of the Year.{{cite web|author=Steven Russo|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/holden-team-year-announced/|title=Holden Cup Team Of The Year Announced|publisher=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2014-09-02|access-date=2015-05-03|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150626101814/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/holden-team-year-announced/|archive-date=26 June 2015|df=dmy-all}} On 8 September 2014, he was named in the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] train-on squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]],{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/09/08/knights_a_plenty_in_samoan_train_on_squad.html|title=Knights a plenty in Samoan train on squad - Knights|date=8 September 2014|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-05-03}} later being also named in the [[Junior Kangaroos]] train-on squad.{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/09/22/nyc_duo_in_junior_kangaroo_train_on_squad.html|title=Young Knights aplenty in rep teams - Knights|date=23 September 2014|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-05-03}} On 12 October 2014, he played for the [[Prime Minister's XIII]] against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/pms-xiii-defeat-valiant-png/tabid/10874/newsid/82486/default.aspx|title=PM's XIII defeat valiant PNG|publisher=NRL.com|date=2014-10-12|access-date=2015-05-03}} On 14 October 2014, he was named in the [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian]] 2014 Four Nations squad.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/holden-kangaroos-2014-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82512/default.aspx|title=Holden Kangaroos 2014 Four Nations squad|publisher=NRL.com|date=2014-10-14|access-date=2015-05-03}} He became Australia's youngest ever Test player when he played against [[England national rugby league team|England]], aged 18 years and 129 days, eclipsing [[Israel Folau]]'s seven-year-old record by 65 days.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/mature-matautia-braced-for-biggest-test/tabid/10874/newsid/82994/default.aspx?cid=NRL_HP_Latest|title=Mature Mata'utia braced for biggest Test|publisher=NRL.com|date=2014-11-13|access-date=2015-05-03}} He played in 3 matches for Australia including playing on the wing and scoring a try in the Kangaroos' 22-18 loss to [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at [[Westpac Stadium]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/nov/15/four-nations-final-new-zealand-australia-report|title=Four Nations final: New Zealand 22-18 Australia | Match report|agency=Australian Associated Press|date=15 November 2014|via=www.theguardian.com}} Late in September 2014, he signed a letter of intent to join the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] on a four-year contract starting in 2016, along with his brothers Chanel and Pat.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/bulldogs-secure-knights-band-of-brothers-sione-chanel-and-pat-matautia/story-fnp0lyn3-1227074495417|title=Bulldogs secure Knights' band of brothers Sione, Chanel and Pat Mata'utia|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-05-03}}{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/newcastle-knights-rising-star-sione-matautia-to-let-mum-decide-his-long-term-playing-future/story-fnp0lyn3-1227165380318|title=Newcastle Knights rising star Sione Mata'utia to let mum decide his long-term playing future|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-05-03}} However, the NRL's rules didn't allow Canterbury to register the contracts until 30 June 2015, leaving the option of staying at Newcastle open for the Mata'utias.{{cite web|last=Keeble|first=Brett|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/2774580/agent-tussle-holds-up-matautia-talks/|title=Agent tussle holds up Mata'utia talks | Newcastle Herald|publisher=Theherald.com.au|date=2014-12-18|access-date=2015-05-03}} ===2015=== On 31 January and 1 February, Mata'utia played for Newcastle in the [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines]].{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-2015-nines-squad/|title=KNIGHTS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2015-01-20|access-date=2015-12-21|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160130234832/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-2015-nines-squad/|archive-date=30 January 2016|df=dmy-all}} On 19 March, he re-signed with Newcastle on a three-year contract, along with his brothers Chanel and Pat.{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/03/19/mata_utia_brothers_r.html|title=Mata'utia brothers re-sign - Knights|date=18 March 2015|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-05-03}}{{cite web|author=Brett Keeble|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/newcastle-knights/sione-matautia-and-brothers-chanel-and-pat-resign-with-knights-20150318-1m2mdf.html|title=Sione Mata'utia and brothers Chanel and Pat re-sign with Knights|website=Smh.com.au|date=18 March 2015|access-date=2015-12-21}} He finished off the [[2015 NRL season|2015 season]] having played in 22 matches and scoring 3 tries as the club finished last.{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY2MC00LS0tLS0tMjA4NjYtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-10-18|access-date=2015-12-25}} ===2016=== In February, Mata'utia played for Newcastle in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]].{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-announce-nines-squad/|title=Knights Announce Nines Squad - Rugby League Week|website=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2016-01-28|access-date=2016-02-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160204042513/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-announce-nines-squad/|archive-date=4 February 2016|df=dmy-all}} In round 17 against the [[Canberra Raiders]], he switched from the centres to second-row in Newcastle's 21-25 golden-point extra time loss at [[Canberra Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/footy-form/knights-sione-matautias-future-is-as-backrower-says-coach-nathan-brown/news-story/079f5c85699636a7385346ac692f67ff|title=Knights Sione Mata'utia's future is as backrower, says coach Nathan Brown|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|date=2016-07-04|access-date=2016-09-07}}{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/jordan-rapana-try-seals-raiders-goldenpoint-victory-against-knights/news-story/2e787ac7adbfcf1758a8060b47002660|title=Jordan Rapana try seals Raiders' golden-point victory against Knights|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|date=2016-07-03|access-date=2016-09-07}} This would later prove to be a permanent move for the rest of the season.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/footy-form/knights-sione-matautias-future-is-as-backrower-says-coach-nathan-brown/news-story/079f5c85699636a7385346ac692f67ffwww.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/knights/knights-star-sione-matautia-played-almost-20-minutes-with-a-fractured-jaw/news-story/42a08a3488d727f905526dfb4e3b5636|title=Knights star Sione Mata'utia played almost 20 minutes with a fractured jaw|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|date=2016-07-19|access-date=2016-09-07}} He finished the [[2016 NRL season|2016 season]] having played in 16 matches and scoring 4 tries.{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY3Ny00LS0tLS0tMjA4NjYtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2016-09-07}} ===2017=== In round 8 of the [[2017 NRL season|2017 season]], Mata'utia captained Newcastle for the first time in their loss to [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]]. He played a total of 22 games for Newcastle in the [[2017 NRL season]] as the club finished last for a third straight year.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/12/12/knights-2017-by-the-numbers/|title=Knights: 2017 by the numbers|website=NRL|date=12 December 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-cowboys-v-knights/tabid/10874/newsid/105879/default.aspx |title=Updated team lists: Cowboys v Knights |publisher=NRL.com |date=2016-12-18 |access-date=2017-04-22}} ===2018=== In June, Mata'utia re-signed with Newcastle on a three-year contract until the end of 2021.{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/just-in-knights-re-sign-centre-34264/|title=Knights re-sign Sione Mata'utia - Zero Tackle|date=28 June 2018|access-date=29 June 2018}} ===2019=== Mata'utia played 22 games and scored five tries for Newcastle in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club finished 11th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://nothingbutleague.com/2019/10/03/2019-season-review-newcastle-knights/|title=2019 SEASON REVIEW NEWCASTLE KNIGHTS|website=Nothing But League|date=3 October 2019}} ===2020=== Mata'utia had an interrupted 2020 season due to injury, only playing in 12 matches for Newcastle.{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTk2NS00LS0tLS0tMjA4NjYtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}} After 124 games for the Knights, he was released from the final year of his contract in November to take up a three-year contract with [[Super League]] club [[St Helens R.F.C.]] starting in 2021.{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2020/11/11/sione-matautia-granted-immediate-release/|title=Sione Mata'utia granted immediate release|date=11 November 2020|website=Newcastle Knights}}{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12164327/super-league-the-nrl-recruits-moving-to-these-shores-for-the-2021-season/|title=The NRL recruits moving to these shores for the 2021 season|website=www.skysports.com}} ===2021=== In round 1 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he made his debut for St. Helens in their 29-6 victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/mar/26/st-helens-salford-super-league-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Lomax leads way as St Helens make light work of Salford in season opener|website=www.theguardian.com|date=26 March 2021}} On 14 July, Mata'utia was suspended for two matches and missed St. Helens Challenge Cup final victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57818540|title=St Helens forward to miss Challenge Cup final after tackle ban upheld|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 9 October, he played for St. Helens in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Catalans Dragons]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} ===2022=== In round 19 of the 2022 Super League season, he was sent off for a professional foul in St Helens 25-0 victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62147667|title=Super League: St Helens 25-0 Huddersfield Giants - 12-man Saints battle to victory|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 24 September, he played for St Helens in their [[2022 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|website=www.theguardian.com}} ===2023=== On 18 February 2023, he played in St Helens 13-12 upset victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] in the [[2023 World Club Challenge]].{{cite news |title=St Helens stun Penrith to win World Club Challenge |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64666651 |work=BBC Sport}} In April, Mata'utia was knocked out during St Helens loss against arch-rivals Wigan where he remained motionless on the pitch for almost ten minutes and required oxygen. Mata'utia was then ruled out for an indefinite period after being stretchered from the field.{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-sione-matautia-stretchered-26656289/|title=St Helens' Sione Mata'utia stretchered from the field requiring oxygen after knockout|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk}} In the 2023 Challenge Cup semi-final, Mata'utia was controversially sent to the sin bin during St Helens 12-10 loss against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]].{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/explained-why-sione-matautia-wasnt-shown-red-card-78898/|title=Why Sione Mata’utia wasn’t shown red card|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}} He played 20 games for St Helens in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished third on the table. He played in St Helens narrow loss against the Catalans Dragons in the semi-final which stopped them reaching a fifth successive grand final.{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23856044.paul-wellens-q-saints-review-year-look-2024/|title=Paul Wellens Q&A: Saints review of year and a look to 2024|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}} ===2024=== On 11 September, Mata'utia announced he would be departing St Helens at the end of the 2024 Super League season.{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/24577843.sione-matautia-leave-st-helens-return-australia/|title=Sione Mata'utia to leave St Helens and return to Australia|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}} He played 21 games for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table. Mata'utia played in St Helens golden point extra-time playoff loss against Warrington which was also his last game for the side.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title= ‘Soul destroying’: Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}} On November 1, Mata'utia announced he was retiring from professional rugby league after 11 seasons.{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=2024-11-01 |title=Ex-St Helens stalwart Sione Mata’utia confirms retirement |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-st-helens-stalwart-sione-matautia-confirms-retirement/ |access-date=2024-11-01 |website=Total Rugby League |language=en-US}} On 6 Nov 2024 it was reported that he, alongside his brother [[Peter Mata'utia|Peter]], had signed for [[Newcastle Rugby League]] side [[Lakes United Seagulls]]{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=6 Nov 2024|title=Ex-St Helens forward Sione Mata’utia and former Castleford and Warrington man Peter Mata’utia sign for new club|author=Callum Walker|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-st-helens-forward-sione-matautia-and-former-castleford-and-warrington-man-peter-matautia-sign-for-new-club/}} == Statistics == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Year !Team !Games !Tries !Pts |- |2014 | rowspan="7" |{{Leagueicon|Newcastle|16}} [[Newcastle Knights]] |7 |7 |28 |- |2015 |22 |3 |12 |- |2016 |16 |4 |16 |- |2017 |22 |3 |12 |- |2018 |23 |8 |32 |- |2019 |22 |5 |20 |- |2020 |12 |1 |4 |- |2021 | rowspan="4" |{{leagueicon|St Helens|16}} [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]] |22 |5 |20 |- |2022 |16 |5 |20 |- |2023 |24 |2 |8 |- |2024 |15 |4 |16 |- | |Totals |203 |47 |188 |} ==References== {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} ==External links== *[https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/newcastle-knights/sione-matautia/ Newcastle Knights profile] {{St Helens -
